channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Jazz commit 29 turnovers in 133-106 loss to OKC
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Jazz lead the NBA in turnovers, and Tuesday night was their worst night yet. Utah committed a season-high 29 turnovers, 19 in the first half, as the Jazz go 0-4 in NBA Cup games with a 133-106 blowout loss at Oklahoma City.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Wiggins, Schieffelin and Clemson's defense hold down No. 4 Kentucky for a 70-66 win
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Chauncey Wiggins scored 12 points and Ian Schieffelin had 11 points and a career-high 20 rebounds as Clemson shut down No. 4 Kentucky for a 70-66 victory Tuesday night in the SEC/ACC Challenge. The Tigers (8-1) held the Wildcats (7-1) to 30 points fewer than their average of 96.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cougars go cold in 83-64 loss at Providence
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (ABC4 Sports) - The BYU basketball team suffered its worst shooting performance of the season and lost at Providence Tuesday night, 83-64 to fall to 6-2 on the season. The Cougars shot just 33.3 percent from the field in scoring a season-low 64 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa State and SMU athletic directors in a 'stay off my lawn' moment regarding latest rankings
item.description: Athletic directors at Iowa State and SMU are in a turf war over the latest College Football Playoff rankings, leading to a “stay off my lawn" moment on social media. The background: SMU, at 11-1 and riding a nine-game winning streak into the Atlantic Coast Conference title game, was slotted at No.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lincoln's big win, two-way tie lead exciting Gala Awards
item.description: An incredible year for Lincoln quarterback Akili Smith Jr. continued inside the Rock Church Tuesday when he became the second Hornet to win the Silver Pigskin trophy in its 26-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Goldin helps Michigan beat No. 11 Wisconsin 67-64 as Badgers take their 1st loss
item.description: Vladislav Goldin scored a career-high 24 points – including three straight baskets inside down the stretch – and Michigan defeated No. 11 Wisconsin 67-64 in the Big Ten opener for both teams.
item.dc:creator: Steve Megargee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Knicks beat Magic to advance in the NBA Cup. Orlando also moves on as the East wild card
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns had 23 points and 15 rebounds, Jalen Brunson scored 21 points and the New York Knicks beat the Orlando Magic 121-106 on Tuesday to reach the quarterfinals of the NBA Cup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alabama stays alive in penultimate CFP rankings of 2024 as Miami faces trouble
item.description: The College Football Playoff committee released the penultimate rankings of the season as Alabama remains in the picture despite three losses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan football fires offensive coordinator amid disappointing season
item.description: After an inept offensive performance by the offense during the 2024 season, the Michigan Wolverines head coach Sherrone Moore has fired offensive coordinator Kirk Campbell.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carr, Local 4

item.title: Bill Belichick, Marcus Freeman among favorites to be the next head coach of the Chicago Bears, oddsmakers say
item.description: Former New England Patriots head coach Bill Belichick and current Chicago Bears interim head coach Thomas Brown are among the early favorites to be the Bears' next head coach, according to sports gambling outlets.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: High school recruiting isn't the only way to build a winner in the era of the transfer portal
item.description: Texas A&M signed the nation’s top-ranked recruiting class three years ago believing it had built a potential national title contender. Plenty of players from that heralded 2022 class could indeed be participating in the first 12-team College Football Playoff this month.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philadelphia ready to go the distance with RockyFest week dedicated to 'Rocky' movies
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Rocky Balboa fans are ready to go the distance — by bus, by ice skates, by 72 steps — to honor Philly’s favorite fictional fighter almost 50 years after the first movie launched the enduring series of an underdog boxer persevering despite the odds.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL's Goodell and Commanders' Harris meet with lawmakers to talk about potential RFK stadium site
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell and Washington Commanders controlling owner Josh Harris met with congressional lawmakers Monday on Capitol Hill as efforts push forward to secure access to the RFK Stadium site in the District of Columbia.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel Vázquez, the three-time world boxing champion from Mexico, dies at 46
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Israel Vázquez, the Mexican fighter who won three bantamweight world titles, has died at 46. World Boxing Council president Mauricio Sulaiman announced the death on Tuesday. The boxer nicknamed “El Magnifico” revealed on Nov. 10 he had sarcoma, a cancer.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fueled by boos, Browns receiver Jerry Jeudy turns in 235-yard performance in return to Denver
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The boos only brought out the best in Jerry Jeudy as he returned to Denver. "They only boo you,” Jeudy reasoned, “when they know there’s something great in you.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ESPN star Stephen A Smith tells Democrats to 'shut up' after election failure, spending scandal
item.description: ESPN star Stephen A. Smith told Democrats they needed to "shut up" after a demoralizing election defeat at the hands of President-elect Donald Trump and Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Report: Michigan seeking to play Northwestern at Soldier Field in 2025
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — College football season is over for Northwestern University, but that hasn't stopped higher-ups in the administration from weighing venue options for one of its high profile matchups in 2025. According to rg.org's Paul M.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Lou Carnesecca, New York basketball legend, passes away at 99
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Lou Carnesecca is a New York legend. Carnesecca passed away over the weekend at the age of 99. He lived a wonderful life, and his impact was unquestioned on and off the basketball court. Born on Jan. 5, 1925, he was the son of Italian immigrants. He attended St.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: 2024 PPR All 51 Team
item.description: 2024 PPR All 51 Team: Each season our selection committee recognizes the best players from across the county, assembling our "All 51" team. This list includes our first team picks, and honorable mentions.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Worley, Fox 5

item.title: McLaughlin's field goal in OT helps Bucs beat Panthers 26-23, move into 1st-place tie in NFC South
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers beat the Carolina Panthers 26-23 in overtime on a 30-yard field goal by Chase McLaughlin on Sunday to move into a tie for first place in the NFC South.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vikings win 5th straight, rallying past Cardinals 23-22 on Sam Darnold's TD pass with 1:13 left
item.description: Sam Darnold's second touchdown pass of the second half went to Jones for Minnesota's first lead with 1:13 remaining, and the Vikings rallied from 13 points down to beat the Arizona Cardinals 23-22 on Sunday for their fifth straight victory.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers extend contract of postseason hero
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers have agreed to a contract extension with one of the key pieces of the team's World Series title run. The Dodgers reached agreement Friday on a deal with Tommy Edman, ensuring that the do-it-all utility-man will remain with the team for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Spurgeon breaks 33-game scoreless streak with two goals, Minnesota beats Chicago 3-2
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Jared Spurgeon ended a 33-game scoring drought with two of Minnesota’s three second-period goals and the Wild beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Friday. Marco Rossi also scored for Minnesota and Marc-Andre Fleury, who turned 40 on Thursday, made 20 saves to improve to 5-0-1.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama A&M player Medrick Burnett Jr. dies following injury during game
item.description: Medrick Burnett Jr., a 20-year-old football player for Alabama A&M University, died Wednesday a month after sustaining a serious injury during a football game.
item.dc:creator: Drew Taylor, NewsNation

item.title: Jon Batiste, Ledisi, Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle to perform during Super Bowl pregame
item.description: The Super Bowl pregame will have some Louisiana flavor: Multi-talented performer Jon Batiste will hit the stage to sing the national anthem, while Trombone Shorty and Lauren Daigle are slated to perform “America the Beautiful.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boise State withdraws from Mountain West volleyball tournament rather than play San Jose State
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Boise State, which twice boycotted regular-season matches with San Jose State, pulled out of the Mountain West women's volleyball tournament Wednesday night even after securing a spot in the semifinals against the Spartans.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bears CB Jaylon Johnson not interested in bright spots with team's skid at 5 games going into Thanksgiving
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. (AP) — Jaylon Johnson wasn't all that interested in discussing any bright spots or reasons to have hope for the Bears. The star cornerback made his feelings clear.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: 2 companies cut ties with Conor McGregor
item.description: Proximo Spirits, the owner of Irish whiskey brand Proper No. 12, will no longer feature McGregor’s name.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
